_schema: Curated, machine-readable registry of audit finding IDs that are surfaced in USER-FACING copy (currently src/lib/featureCatalogue.js). Each entry is the canonical current status of that finding, reviewed by a human against the cited `source` audit doc. It exists so a terse user-facing surface can never silently contradict the audit (the 2026-07-06 C-1 drift: the catalogue called a RESOLVED finding an 'open CRITICAL'). scripts/check-finding-id-consistency.mjs enforces two things: (1) no user-facing surface describes a `status != open` finding as open/unresolved; (2) each finding's `source` doc actually contains the ID. This registry is the source of truth for the GATE, not for the audit itself — when an audit doc changes a finding's status, update this file in the same PR. Invariants (I1-I6), roadmap milestones (M2c/M2d), dApp transport steps (D1-D3), and mitigation labels (M-K) are NOT audit findings and are intentionally absent.

_status_vocabulary.open: unresolved; may legitimately be described as open in user-facing copy
_status_vocabulary.fixed: remediated and merged; must NOT be described as open/unresolved/an open finding
_status_vocabulary.accepted: documented accepted deviation (won't-fix by decision); must NOT be described as an open finding
_status_vocabulary.evidence-gap: code-fixed but a device/on-chain trace is still outstanding; treat like fixed for the openness check

C-1.title: Android StrongBox KEK derived H from a global fixed HMAC input (missing per-enrollment kekSalt binding)
C-1.note: Distinct from the StrongBox tier-ENFORCEMENT gap (reject non-StrongBox devices), which is a TARGET, not a numbered finding. Do not conflate the two — that conflation was the 2026-07-06 drift.

H-1.title: Hardware KEK StrongBox tier was not surfaced honestly in the badge/UI

M-4.title: RASP send-path wiring was present but stale 'NOT WIRED' comments implied otherwise

L-1.title: Encrypted personal backup PIN floor was too low (4 digits); raised to 6
